what_it_does: Builds a two-panel explanatory HTML screen for a unit face: the left side renders a live DNA unit visualization from hard-coded sample record data, and the right side explains what the unit face represents and what actions the user can take.
observations: Uses Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono via Google Fonts plus shared surface CSS files.
Defines a split flex layout with a left visualization zone and right explanatory content area.
Loads /surface/runtime/organisms.js and /surface/runtime/unit-view.js, then uses the global DNA API.
Creates a hard-coded capture record for a code source address and turns it into a rendered unit via DNA.unitFromCorpus and DNA.renderUnit.
Lists five user actions: Navigate, Annotate, Ask, Drive, and Open the source.
Adds a consequences box labelled 'What this is, counted' with four numeric facts.
standouts: Combines a rendered interactive-looking unit face with plain-language explanatory copy in the same file.
The sample unit is a code artifact, presented as a constellation of related files with scores and roles.
The right panel frames the bottom-right V as the action locus for the face.
conventions: Keeps styling file-local in an inline style block while relying on shared surface variables and runtime scripts.
Uses semantic explanatory HTML content with custom design-system classes such as superhead, bp, and foot.
Uses hard-coded demo data directly inside the page script.
Uses abbreviated code addresses with an ellipsis character in neighbour records.
concerns: The page depends on global DNA runtime objects without local guards or fallback behavior.
Hard-coded source paths include a user-specific /home/tim address.
Some described capabilities, such as live dials and built-in AI, are not implemented directly in this file and depend on external runtime behavior.
notes: A file-local explainer page that demonstrates and narrates a unit face using fixed sample data.
questions: What behavior is attached to the bottom-right V by the external unit runtime?
Are the abbreviated neighbour addresses intended as display-only values or usable source addresses?
Should this file remain a fixed demo or receive its record data from a ledger/source pipeline?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it explains a unit face by showing a rendered example and adjacent explanatory actions.
apparent_themes: code artifact as unit of memory
explanation through live specimen plus annotations
design-system composition around reusable DNA surfaces
intention_signals: It seems intended to make the abstract unit-face concept legible by pairing a concrete rendered example with concise user-facing affordance descriptions.
novelty: The file treats a code pipeline record as a visual memory object and presents related files as a constellation with action semantics around a marked V control.
contribution: Contributes an explanatory specimen screen for a unit face, including layout, sample record data, relation metadata, action language, and counted consequences that a fused system could preserve as a demonstration pattern.
summary_for_embedding: This is an HTML explainer for a DNA-rendered unit face, using hard-coded Distill pipeline sample data to show a constellation-style code memory view with related units and action descriptions.
intention_for_embedding: This was for teaching what a unit face is and how a user can navigate, annotate, ask about, drive, and open the source behind a unit.
